body: PT Satria Andalan Prima Tbk (SAPX) targets 25-30% revenue growth this year, aiming to reach IDR 750-800 billion, driven by its focus on corporate clients and cost efficiency in its core business activities. In September 2024, SAPX's revenue grew 16.58% year-on-year to IDR 523.35 billion, but net profit plummeted 86.5% due to rising operational costs, and the company plans to launch an advanced payment system and integrate its internal warehouse to achieve its revenue growth target.
title: PT Satria Andalan Prima Targets 25-30% Revenue Growth This Year Focusing on Corporate Clients.

body: Foreign investors, including 1st Financial Company Limited and HSBC-Fund SVS A/C Chengdong Investment Corp-Self, reduced their stakes in PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BUMI) and PT Bumi Resources Tbk, respectively, while PT DWimuria Investama Andalan acquired 396.27 million shares of PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk (TOWR), increasing its ownership to 6.37%. Other notable transactions include PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk (EKTG) increasing its stake in a television broadcasting company to approximately 62%, and PT Pangan Sejahtera Investama expanding its stake in PT FKS Food Sejahtera Tbk (FKSS).
title: Multiple foreign and local investors adjust their stakes in various Indonesian companies, including PT Bumi Resources Minerals, PT MNC Digital Entertainment, and PT Sarana Menara Nusantara.

body: PT Kentanix Supra International Tbk (KSIX) has officially listed its shares on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, with 61.5% of the IPO proceeds to be used for working capital and infrastructure development, including construction of houses in two existing projects and a new project expansion. On its inaugural trade, the company's shares surged 13.94% to IDR 515 per share from the initial price of IDR 452 per share, eventually touching the upper limit of 25% increase at IDR 565 per share.
title: PT Kentanix Supra International Tbk lists shares on Indonesia Stock Exchange, raises funds for infrastructure development and housing projects.

body: PT Raharja Energy Cepu TBK (RATU) raised IDR 624 billion from its initial public offering of 543,010,800 shares at IDR 1,150 per share, with its shares surging 24.78% to IDR 1,435 in its first trading session. As of Q2 2024, the company's total assets stood at USD 50.62 million, total liabilities at USD 29.74 million, and total equity at USD 20.89 million, with the IPO aimed at strengthening its position and expanding its business network.
title: Raharja Energy Cepu's shares surge 24.78% on first trading day after successful IPO.

body: Indonesia has officially joined the BRICS economic bloc, which aims to reform global governance institutions and promote cooperation among developing countries, with plans to increase international transactions using currencies other than the US dollar. The move is expected to benefit Indonesia by providing a platform to advocate for its interests in multilateral forums and access to the New Development Bank, a financial institution supporting developing countries.
title: Indonesia officially joins BRICS economic bloc, expected to benefit from advocacy and cooperation on global governance and development issues.

body: PT Aspirasi Hidup Indonesia Tbk (ACES) has extended a loan of IDR 250 billion to its subsidiary, PT Kawan Lama Inovasi (KLV), at a 7% interest rate to support KLV's working capital and business operations. This transaction is expected to contribute to Aces' overall performance, which has seen a 13.5% year-on-year revenue growth to IDR 6.1 trillion and an 18.1% increase in net profit to IDR 574.2 billion in Q3 2024.
title: PT Aspirasi Hidup Indonesia Extends Loan to Subsidiary PT Kawan Lama Inovasi for Working Capital Purposes.

body: PT Tanah Laut Tbk (INDX) is implementing a palm downstream strategy to optimize Indonesia's rich palm resources, aiming to improve community welfare and deliver added value to shareholders. The company has secured support from the Indonesian Palm Council and is finalizing land purchase options to strengthen its supply chain, enhance production capacity, and diversify its product lines, expecting a positive impact on growth and increased value for shareholders.
title: PT Tanah Laut Tbk to Develop Indonesia's Palm Sector through Downstream Strategy

body: PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) is closing its marketplace for physical goods, ceasing operations and disabling new product listings from February 1, 2025, to focus on selling virtual products and digital services. The company will automatically cancel unprocessed orders by March 2, 2025, and process refunds through Bukadompet, shifting its business to transactions like prepaid phone credit, electricity tokens, and other digital services.
title: Bukalapak to cease physical product sales on its marketplace, shifting focus to virtual products and digital services.

body: Bank credit in Indonesia grew 10.79% in November 2024, reaching IDR 7,717 trillion, with investment banking loans recording the highest growth at 13.77%, followed by consumer credit and working capital credit. Meanwhile, third-party funds collected by banks grew 7.54% year-over-year to IDR 8,836 trillion, with asset quality remaining good, as reflected in a gross non-performing loan ratio of 2.19% and net NPL of 0.75%.
title: Bank Credit in Indonesia Sees 10.79% Growth in November 2024, Maintaining Positive Performance.

body: Agatha Nindya sold 58.74 million shares of PT Techno9 Indonesia TBK (TECH), reducing her ownership stake from 13.35% to 10.64%, while PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi TBK (ELMT) acquired 29.50 million shares of PT Surya Citra Media TBK, marking the largest share acquisition in the market hints. Other notable transactions include sales of shares in PT Hillcon TBK, PT Wilton Makmur Indonesia TBK, PT Bumi Resources TBK, and PT Bank Ina Perdana TBK, as well as purchases of shares in PT Kota Satu Properti TBK, PT Tempo Scan Pacific TBK, and Trias Sentosa TBK.
title: Multiple Investors Engage in Significant Share Transactions in Indonesian Companies

body: PT Darma Henwa Tbk (DEWA) is working to eliminate its accumulated loss of Rp1.08 trillion by offsetting currency adjustment balances against negative retained earnings, with the company still conducting an internal review of its currency net-off plan. As of its third-quarter 2024 financial report, DEWA recorded currency adjustments of Rp1.46 trillion and a deficit or negative retained earnings of Rp1.08 trillion.
title: Darma Henwa Continues Efforts to Eliminate Rp1.08 Trillion Deficit through Currency Adjustment Balances.

body: PT Bangun Karya Perkasa Jaya Tbk (KRYA) has secured a contract worth IDR 23.87 billion from PT Niagatama Kencana, a subsidiary of Wings Group, to build a warehouse project called Depo Tomo, which is expected to be completed within eight months. This is not the first collaboration between KRYA and Wings Group, as KRYA previously worked on Wings Group's nondairy creamer milk powder factory project worth IDR 14.19 billion.
title: PT Bangun Karya Perkasa Jaya secures Idr 23.87 billion contract to build Depo Tomo warehouse for Wings Group subsidiary.

body: PT Citra Nusantara Gemilang Tbk (CGAS) shares have fallen 69.82% to IDR 102 per share from IDR 338 per share since its initial public offering on January 8, 2024, with a trading volume of 2.50 million shares worth IDR 256.50 million. The company raised IDR 179.62 billion from the IPO, with 90% allocated for capital expenditure to finance the construction of a liquefied natural gas station and 10% for working capital.
title: PT Citra Nusantara Gemilang's Share Price Plummets 69.82% to IDR 102 per Share After One Year of Listing.

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange suspended trading of PT Citra Putra Realty Tbk (CLAY) shares due to low trading activity and liquidity over the past three months, triggered by a 9.77% price drop to RP240 from RP266. The company's shares exhibited low liquidity, with an average daily transaction value of less than RP5 million and an average daily trading volume of fewer than 10,000 shares over the past three months.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Suspends Trading of PT Citra Putra Realty Shares Due to Low Liquidity and Price Drop.

body: Soul Parking, an Indonesian parking management service provider, has concluded its Series A+ funding round led by AppWorks and AC Ventures, with participation from other investors, although the amount raised remains undisclosed. The new capital will support Soul Parking's market improvement objectives and expansion plans to several countries, building on its existing business foundation and promising growth potential in the parking industry.
title: Soul Parking concludes Series A+ funding round to support market improvement and expansion plans.

body: PT Kereta Api Indonesia (none) refinanced its 2017 and 2019 shelf registration bonds with a total value of IDR 1.49 trillion, allocating 67% to principal debt of its 2017 bonds and 33% to principal debt of its 2019 bonds. The refinancing was done through the issuance of Shelf Registration Bond II Phase I 2024, worth IDR 1.5 trillion, with net proceeds of IDR 1.49 trillion after excluding public offering costs of IDR 7.48 billion.
title: PT Kereta Api Indonesia refinances IDR 1.49 trillion in bonds from 2017 and 2019 with new IDR 1.5 trillion issuance.
